result.summary: AfterShip exposes 8 recorded HTTP APIs—Tracking, Returns, Shipping, Commerce, Protection, Parser, Members, Address, and Warranty—authenticated primarily with an AfterShip API key sent in the `as-api-key` header, with Tracking and Shipping also documenting OAuth 2.0 support.

aftership_api_key.label: AfterShip API key
aftership_api_key.setup: Sign in to the AfterShip organization console and create an API key from [API Keys](https://organization.automizely.com/api-keys). The docs repeatedly point developers to this page via the `Log in` link on product API docs. Use the resulting key in the `as-api-key` request header.

aftership_oauth_app.label: AfterShip OAuth app
aftership_oauth_app.setup: For products that support OAuth, follow [Getting Started](https://www.aftership.com/docs/tracking/quickstart/authentication/oauth/getting-started) from the product's docs to register an OAuth app, configure redirect URI(s), and request the needed scopes. Then complete the AfterShip OAuth flow to obtain access tokens for API calls.
